title.value: Zinc-mediated carboxylations of allylic and propargylic halides in flow: synthesis of γ-lactones via subsequent bromolactonization

abstract.value: Zinc-mediated carboxylation of allylic halides under flow conditions delivered β,γ-unsaturated carboxylic acids and subsequent bromolactonization provides a streamlined process for the synthesis of β-bromo-γ-lactones. The described process further demonstrates the utility of organozinc reagents prepared by passage of allylic halides through a metallic zinc column integrated into a flow process. Use of a tube-in-tube reactor for efficient CO2 introduction led to improvements in conversion compared to a batch process and improved overall yields of γ-lactones. The described flow process was also applied to propargylic bromides for the synthesis of allenic and propargylic acids.

warnings: OCR in the supplied snippets dropped several Greek letters; β/γ notation was restored from context in the title and abstract.
Paper has mixed allylic/propargylic scope; allene-focused classification is based on the propargylic bromide to allenic acid portion of the study.
The reaction_type label is approximate because the paper describes CO2 carboxylation rather than a named allene-classification reaction category with an exact match.

value.reaction_type: carbonylative synthesis of allenes and allenoates
